channel.title: Last Page
channel.description: Essays, blogs, reviews, notes and verse by O IV.

item.title: Mother of Democracy
item.description: India proudly calls itself the Mother of Democracy. The real test of that claim is not during elections, but when its own students demand accountability.

item.title: Messi Deserved the Golden Ball
item.description: Sometimes the best player doesn’t lift the trophy. That shouldn’t stop him from being recognised as the best player of the tournament.

item.title: When a Generation Stops Being Afraid
item.description: The real danger for any government is not a protest. It is believing that protests no longer deserve an answer.

item.title: After Estadio Azteca, it's Atlanta
item.description: England and Argentina are about to write another chapter in football's greatest international rivalry.

item.title: untitled
item.description: Last time England faced the hand of the God; this time they face the God himself.

item.title: Ignoring a Protest Is Also a Response
item.description: Democracies are not judged by whether they agree with a protest, but by whether they are willing to answer it.

item.title: Nothing Ends in War and Peace
item.description: Tolstoy wrote a novel about war, history, love, death, and redemption, only to arrive at the unsettling possibility that life never really resolves at all.

item.title: Ankara Messi
item.description: From despair to belief in thirteen unforgettable minutes.

item.title: The Strongest Uchiha Was Never Madara
item.description: If Obito Uchiha had kept both his eyes, Naruto might have accidentally created the most broken character in the entire series

item.title: The Problem Is Not Freebies
item.description: We call welfare a freebie and corporate subsidies an investment, then wonder why nobody can have an honest conversation about economics.

item.title: What If Walter Pater Was Right?
item.description: Art does not owe us morality, but society has a habit of stealing morality from art anyway

item.title: The Last Dance
item.description: The final World Cup of football’s greatest generation

item.title: The Day I Realized I Hate Arsenal
item.description: Just a club whose defeats seem to bring me an unreasonable amount of joy.
